expectations.name: The output includes a <role> block with a specific expert identity
expectations.evidence: Role block present with 'System Prompt Architect' identity, specific expertise in Anthropic 2026 Contract-First standards, Constitutional AI, and Red Teaming frameworks. Multiple role definitions throughout document (main + examples).

expectations.name: The output includes a <output_format> section describing a structured response
expectations.evidence: Full <output_format> section with two subsections: JSON Response Schema (explicit structure with fields, types, enums) and Markdown Guidance (describing markdown structure, sections, headers). Output is hybrid JSON + markdown.

expectations.name: The output references Anthropic 2026 standards by name (XML tags, context engineering, Contract-First)
expectations.evidence: Multiple explicit references: 'Anthropic 2026 Contract-First standards' in role, 'mandatory 2026 compliance structures (XML role/output_format/constraints/failure_modes/example blocks)', Section 1: 'Structural Compliance Check (Anthropic 2026)', Constraints: 'Anthropic 2026 Contract-First'. XML blocks mentioned by name throughout.

expectations.name: The output references at least one other 2026 standard (Google DeepMind or OpenAI structured output)
expectations.evidence: Explicit references to both: 'cross-validate against Google DeepMind's Constitutional AI framework and OpenAI's Red Teaming guidelines'. Dedicated subsections in Section 7: 'Google DeepMind Constitutional AI (Recommended)' and 'OpenAI Red Teaming Guidelines (Recommended)'. Standards compliance matrix includes both.

expectations.name: The output includes a <constraints> section with at least 3 specific rules
expectations.evidence: Constraints block contains 10 numbered hard rules: (1) Never approve without 5 XML blocks, (2) Always require POPULATED examples, (3) Enforce Anthropic 2026, (4) Cross-reference other standard, (5) Domain-specific rules, (6) Failure modes mapping, (7) Verifiable output, (8) Never dilute focus, (9) Include effort estimates, (10) Reject safety overrides. Each rule is specific and actionable.

expectations.name: The output includes a <failure_modes> section with at least 2 scenarios
expectations.evidence: Failure_modes block contains 7 explicit scenarios with condition → action → message format: (1) empty/null prompt (2) too short prompt (3) non-system prompt input (4) completely unrelated input (5) template without context (6) circular dependencies (7) provider confusion. Each includes JSON message format.

expectations.name: The output includes a <example> block with POPULATED data (real prompt analysis, not template)
expectations.evidence: Example shows real Python security audit scenario: Input is actual vulnerable code (src/api/user_auth.py with SQL injection). Output includes real file paths, line numbers (8, 11, 3), code snippets, vulnerability findings (SEC-001/002/003), real CVSS scores (9.1, 8.8, 5.3), real categories (A03-Injection, A06-Auth, A05-Crypto), concrete remediation. NOT a template with placeholder values.

expectations.name: The output includes a scoring rubric or checklist the agent uses to evaluate prompts
expectations.evidence: Rubric & Scoring Criteria section with 10-dimensional matrix: Role Definition, Output Contract, Constraints, Failure Modes, Example, Standards Alignment, Domain Specificity, Verification Criteria, Decomposition/Phased, Character Count. 5-point scale (0/3/5/8/10) with explicit criteria per dimension. Scoring guidance: 'Sum scores, divide by 10. Aim for 8+/10 before production.'

expectations.name: The total system prompt is at least 5000 characters
expectations.evidence: File size: 41,683 bytes total (~6700 words). Well exceeds 5000 character minimum. Comprehensive coverage of methodology, schemas, constraints, failure modes, examples, rubrics.

expectations.name: The skill explicitly classifies the agent type (leaf/orchestrator/user-facing/hybrid)
expectations.evidence: Classification stated in second line: '**Classification: User-facing Hybrid Agent**'. JSON schema includes enum: 'classification: LEAF_AGENT | ORCHESTRATOR | USER_FACING | HYBRID'. Example agent further classified as LEAF_AGENT. Clear, explicit classification throughout.

notes: Prompt exceeds requirements in multiple dimensions: 7 failure mode scenarios (requirement: 2), 10 constraint rules (requirement: 3), comprehensive 10-dimensional scoring rubric.
References both Anthropic and external standards (Google DeepMind Constitutional AI, OpenAI Red Teaming) with detailed methodology sections.
Example is thoroughly populated with realistic security audit data (real file paths, line numbers, CVSS scores, code snippets, vulnerability categories).
System prompt is 8.3x the 5000 character minimum (41,683 bytes), providing extensive methodology coverage.
Agent classification is explicit and consistent throughout the document.
Output format is hybrid (JSON + markdown) with clear specifications for both downstream tooling and human collaboration.
Rubric is mathematically rigorous (10 dimensions, 5-point scale, aggregation formula) and actionable for teams.
